response.justification: Bogdan brings 15+ years of polyglot full-stack experience anchored in TypeScript and Node.js, which maps directly to the primary stack. His current role at re:cinq explicitly involves AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling — a rare and direct match for the role's AI agent development requirement. TDD discipline is deeply embedded: he authored open-source testing frameworks and practiced trunk-based development across multiple senior and staff-level roles. The main gaps are NestJS (not mentioned), Kotlin (absent from the resume), and no explicit callout of Vitest/Jest/Playwright despite strong general testing culture. These are learnable gaps in an otherwise strong profile.
strengths: 15+ years of TypeScript/Node.js full-stack production experience across Staff and Senior roles, including high-throughput and cloud-native (AWS CDK, Kubernetes) systems
Hands-on AI agent infrastructure and MCP code generator experience at current role — directly aligned with the role's AI agent development requirement
Deeply embedded testing culture: authored open-source testing libraries (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), practiced TDD and trunk-based development across multiple employers
concerns: NestJS is entirely absent from the resume despite being the primary backend framework for this role
Kotlin is not mentioned anywhere; the JD lists it as a required secondary technology
React experience is thin — mentioned once at Optilyz with no detail on component architecture, state management, or significant frontend ownership

key_factors.factor: AI agent and MCP tooling experience
